summary: Retire the orphaned hand-TOML clause-table parser (`Contract::parse` + helpers) — live floors build from lock ClauseRows; only tests reach it

per.section: The clause — the atom of a contract

edit.description: Remove the hand-TOML clause-table parser and everything only it reaches: `Contract::parse` (line 572) plus `parse_clauses`, `parse_clause`, `parse_guidance`, `parse_source`, `parse_severity`, `parse_predicate`, `parse_charset`, `parse_range`, `parse_edge_bound`, and the now-unused `ContractError` enum (line 441 — every variant is a TOML-parse error: Toml/ClauseNotArray/MissingParam/WrongType/UnknownSeverity/UnknownPredicate/UnknownKey/UnknownType/InvalidRange). Delete the parser's in-module tests (the 25 `Contract::parse` sites after line 1008). Keep the `Contract`/`Clause`/`Predicate` types (the live gate's in-memory shapes) and `Predicate::arg_keys` unless it goes dead with the parser. Fix the module doc (line 8) which still says clauses 'parse through the same clause parser `Contract::parse` runs over bare TOML' — the live path builds Contracts from lock `ClauseRow`s via `compose::effective`/`builtin`, never from text. Corpus: 'There is no hand-authored clause grammar — no clause document, no TOML clause table; the SDK's types are the only authoring spelling.'

Drop the parser-exercising assertions (the 7 `Contract::parse` sites at lines 233/278/294/320/353/366/382 pinning malformed-TOML rejection and skill-TOML parse) — they test the retired hand-TOML surface. KEEP the built-in floor pin loaded via `temper::builtin::contract("skill")` (the embedded-lock path the shipped `check` uses) and its `predicate_vector` equality. Fix the stale module-doc cite 'Packages — best practices as data' (no such section in 10-contracts.md).

Port or drop the single `Contract::parse` site (line 144, an error-path assertion) — construct the `Contract`/`Clause` directly, or exercise the `section_contains` predicate through the live lock/compose path, since the TOML parser is retired.

acceptance: `Contract::parse` and its TOML clause-table helpers are gone; `cargo build`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` are green with the built-in `skill` floor still pinned by contract_template.rs; every live Contract is built from lock ClauseRows, never from parsed TOML text.
notes: Dead code: `Contract::parse` is reached only from contract.rs's own test module (25 sites) plus 8 sites in contract_template.rs/section_contains.rs. The derived-lock chain (BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION, shipped) rewired live floors to lock rows, orphaning the parser. Watch for clippy dead-code on `ContractError` and the toml_edit `Table` helpers once the parser goes.

summary: Cut false package-noun comment narration + dead section-title cites (`Packages`, `Templates`) from check.rs, kind.rs, graph.rs, document.rs, builtin.rs

per.section: Decision: clause arrays and two quantifiers, not a package noun

edit.description: Diagnostic guidance-field doc comments (lines ~200, ~245, ~269) cite a section 'Packages' that no longer exists in 10-contracts.md; the guidance channel is still live, so reword to current vocabulary (clause guidance; 'The clause — the atom of a contract'). Lines ~477/~509 narrate a 'bound `agent` package' — the package noun is dissolved; describe via the requirement's set-scope clauses (membership over a satisfier set) instead. Comment text only, no behavior change.

Package-era narration in CustomKind/Genre doc comments: 'is a package bound in the assembly' (~50), 'the predicates the bound package's' (~90), 'the bound package, out of the kind object' (~161/~171). Reword to the current model — a custom kind is declare-side; predicates ride the assembly's `expect`/`require` clauses (10-contracts). Comment text ONLY: do not rename the live `activation`/`format`/`unit_shape` fields (a separate symbol-rename concern).

Line ~385 doc comment 'never a member's or a package clause's' cites the retired package noun; reword — the reachability severity is the assembly's dial (45-governance, 'The world is a node'), never a package clause's.

Module doc (~17): 'the member and package kinds parse their source into a [Document], read the clause tables out of its header' narrates the retired hand-TOML manifest surface. Document is live only for frontmatter and `+++` kind headers (kind.rs, frontmatter.rs). Reword to what it does — format-preserving document parse/emit — and drop the 'clause tables out of its header' claim.

Line ~93 doc 'No package-level `guidance`' reworded off the package noun — a floor is an exported clause array with per-clause guidance (10-contracts). Comment-only; the prior sweep cut this file's other package refs but left this one.

acceptance: No src comment in check.rs/kind.rs/graph.rs/document.rs/builtin.rs cites a nonexistent 10-contracts.md section ('Packages'/'Templates') or narrates the dissolved package noun as if live; `cargo build`/`clippy`/`test` unchanged (comment-only).
notes: Follow-on to the shipped COMMENT-STOCK-SWEEP (f27bbf4), which cut package-era narration only in the tree/edge files. These are the false package-noun comments in the files it did not cover. Out of scope: renaming the live `activation` type/field to `registration` (separate tick), and stale-form `[[genres]]` manifest narration in kind.rs. install.rs's `SDK_PACKAGE`/`PackageJson` are the npm package.json — legitimate, untouched.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
